content: Just as guided classroom lexical reflection helps high school learners correct misinterpretations of word meanings and build enduring, context-rich vocabulary memory, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow the structured scaffolding of language learning reflection frameworks to systematically root out inconsistent reasoning across outputs. This cross-pollination frames LLM self-reflection not as a novel AI-only technique, but as a formalized iteration of the same reflective learning humans have used for skill building for decades.
